title: drosera indica var. makinoi
intro: Drosera indica var. makinoi, also known as the Asian sundew or Makino's sundew, is a carnivorous plant species belonging to the Drosera genus. It is native to parts of Asia and is known for its ability to capture and digest insects, using its modified leaves to form a sticky droplet of glue-like substance. The medicinal properties of Drosera indica var. makinoi include its potential anti-inflammatory and expectorant effects, which may be beneficial in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a tincture, tea, or syrup, and is used to promote respiratory health and alleviate symptoms of coughs and colds.
